title: Wikstroemia macfadyenii
intro: Wikstroemia macfadyenii, also known as the Ma'o hau hele, is a flowering plant native to Hawaii that belongs to the family Thymelaeaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in wound healing and skin care. The bioactive constituents of Wikstroemia macfadyenii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and topical creams, although its use is primarily limited to traditional Hawaiian medicine and has not been extensively studied or validated by modern Western medicine.
